m_Item1.QuestionText: A train of 145 meters long is running with a speed of 25km/hr. In what time will it pass a man who is walking at  4 km/hr in the direction opposite to that of the train?
m_Item1.Explanation:
Relative speed = (25 + 4 ) Km/h = 29  Km/h

time = D/S = (145 *18)/(29*5)  = 18 sec

m_Item1.QuestionText: A man rides his bicycle 10 km at an average speed of 12 km/hr and again travels 12 km at an average speed of 10 km/hr. What is his average speed for the entire trip approximately?
m_Item1.Explanation:
Total distance travelled = 10 + 12 = 22 km
Time taken to travel 10 km at an average speed of 12 km/hr = distance/speed =10/12 hr
Time taken to travel 12 km at an average speed of 10 km/hr = distance/speed=12/10 hr
Total time taken =10/12+12/10 hr
Average speed = distance/time
=22 / (10/12+12/10)      = 660/61 ≈ 10.8 kmph

m_Item1.QuestionText: A student walking at 10 km/hr reaches destination 15 mins late. Next time the student increases speed by 2 km/hr, but is still 5 mins late. Find the distance of his destination from student’s house.
m_Item1.Explanation:
Distance = speed * time 
Distance is same. So, 10(T+15) = 12(T+5) 
T = 45 mins 
Using in first scenario; distance = 10km/hr * (45+15 mins) 
= 10km/hr * 1 hr = 10 km

m_Item1.QuestionText: An aeroplane covers a certain distance at a speed of 240 kmph in 5 hours. To cover the same distance in 1  2/3 hours, it must travel at a speed of
m_Item1.Explanation:
Speed and time are inversely proportional =Speed ∝ 1Time (when distance is constant)
Here distance is constant and Speed and time are inversely proportional
Speed∝ 1/Time
=Speed1/Speed2 =Time2/Time1
=240/Speed2 = (1 2/3)/5                   =240/Speed2 = (5/3)/5
=Speed2 =240×3 
=720 km/hr

m_Item1.QuestionText: In a flight of 600 km, an aircraft was slowed down due to bad weather. Its average speed for the trip was reduced by 200 km/hr and the time of flight increased by 30 minutes. What is the duration of the flight?
m_Item1.Explanation:
Let the duration of the flight = x hours     Given that distance = 600 km
Speed = Distance/Time=600/x................(Equation1)
Duration of the flight due to the slow down = (x + 30/60) hours=(x+1/2) hours
New speed = 600 / (x + 1/2).....................(Equation2)
From Equations 1 and 2, Reduction in Speed = 600/x – 600 / (x + 1/2)
Given that Reduction in average speed = 200 km/hr 
=600/x – 600 / (x + 1/2)=200
=3/x – 3 / (x + 1/2)=1     =6x+3−6x/ X *(2x+1) = 1
=2X2 + X−3=0...........................................(Equation3)
Solving this, (2x+3)(x−1)=0
=x=1 (Removing the -ve value for x)

m_Item1.QuestionText: A man takes 5 hours 45 min in walking to a certain place and riding back. He would have gained 2 hours by riding both ways. The time he would take to walk both ways is?
m_Item1.Explanation:
Given that time taken for riding both ways will be 2 hours lesser than

the time needed for waking one way and riding back

From this, we can understand that 

time needed for riding one way = time needed for waking one way - 2 hours

Given that time taken in walking one way and riding back = 5 hours 45 min

Hence The time he would take to walk both ways = 5 hours 45 min + 2 hours = 7 hours 45 min

m_Item1.QuestionText: A man covered a certain distance at some speed. If he had moved 3 kmph faster, he would have taken 40 minutes less. If he had moved 2 kmph slower, he would have taken 40 minutes more. What is the distance in km?
m_Item1.Explanation:
Let the distance be x km,   the speed in which he moved = v kmph 

Time taken when moving at normal speed - time taken when moving 3 kmph faster = 40 minutes
=    x/v  − x/(v+3) = 40/60        =  3x / v(v+3)  = 2/3
2v(v+3)=9x................(Equation1)
Time taken when moving 2 kmph slower - Time taken when moving at normal speed  = 40 minutes

= x/v −2  − x/v = 40/60       =   x [1/v−2  − 1/v] = 2/3
=  v(v−2) = 3x................(Equation2)
Equation1 / Equation2
= 2v(v+3) / v(v−2) = 9x/3x   = 2v+6 = 3v−6     = v = 12
Substituting this value of v in Equation 1
= 2×12×15 = 9x      => x = 40
Hence distance = 40 km

m_Item1.QuestionText: Two men, A and B, walk from P to Q, a distance of 21 Km, at 3km/hr and 4km/hr respectively. B reaches Q, returns immediately and meets A at R. find the distance from P to R.
m_Item1.Explanation:
When B meets A at R, B has walked the distance PQ + QR and A the distance PR. 
That is, both of them have together walked twice the distance from P to Q, i.e. 42 km.
The speeds of A: B is 3: 4 and they have travelled 42 km.
Hence, the distance PR travelled by A = 3/7 * 42 = 18 km

m_Item1.QuestionText: If a person walks at 14 km/hr instead of 10 km/hr, he would have walked 20 km more. What is the actual distance travelled by him?
m_Item1.Explanation:
Assume that the person would have covered x km if travelled at 10 km/hr
=Speed = Distance/Time=x/10...............(Equation1)
Give that the person would have covered (x + 20) km if travelled at 14 km/hr

=Speed = Distance/Time=(x+20)/14........(Equation2)
From Equations 1 and 2,   x/10=(x+20)/14    x=50

m_Item1.QuestionText: Excluding stoppages, the speed of a bus is 54 kmph and including stoppages, it is 45 kmph. For how many minutes does the bus stop per hour?
m_Item1.Explanation:
speed of the bus excluding stoppages = 54 kmph

speed of the bus including stoppages = 45 kmph


Loss in speed when including stoppages = 54 - 45 = 9kmph

=> In 1 hour, bus covers 9 km less due to stoppages


Hence, time that the bus stop per hour = time taken to cover 9 km
=distance / speed  = 9/54 hour = 1/6 hour = 60/6 min    =10 min

m_Item1.QuestionText: Walking 6/7th of his usual speed, a man is 12 minutes too late. What is the usual time taken by him to cover that distance?
m_Item1.Explanation:
New speed = 6/7 of usual speed 

Speed and time are inversely proportional.

Hence new time = 7/6 of usual time

Hence, 7/6 of usual time - usual time = 12 minutes

=> 1/6 of usual time = 12 minutes

=> usual time = 12 x 6 = 72 minutes 
= 1 hour 12 minutes

m_Item1.QuestionText: Walking at 25% more than usual speed, a person reaches 15 min. earlier to his office. Find his usual & new time required to reach office.

A carriage driving in a fog passed a man who was walking at the rate of 3km an hour in the same direction. He could see the carriage for 4 minutes and it was visible to him upto a distance of 100m. What was the speed of the carriage?
m_Item1.Explanation:
The distance travelled by the man in 4 minutes  = 3000/60 * 4 mtrs = 200 mtrs
So, distance travelled by the carriage in 4 minutes = 200+100 = 300 mtrs 
Speed of the carriage = 300/4 * 60/1000 km/hr = 9/2 = 4.5 km per hour

m_Item1.QuestionText: A and B walk around a circular track. A and B walk at a speed of 2 rounds per hour and 3 rounds per hour respectively. If they start at 8 a.m. from the same point in opposite directions, how many times shall they cross each other before 9.30 a.m.?
m_Item1.Explanation:
Relative speed = Speed of A + Speed of B (∴ they walk in opposite directions)   
= 2 + 3 = 5 rounds per hour

=> They cross each other 5 times in 1 hour and 2 times in 1/2 hour

Time duration from 8 am to 9.30 am = 1.5 hour

Hence they cross each other 7 times before 9.30 am

m_Item1.QuestionText: When a man travels equal distance at speeds V1 and V2 km/hr, his average speed is 4km/hr. But, when he travels at these speeds for equal times his average speed is 4.5km/hr. Find the difference of the two speeds.
m_Item1.Explanation:
Suppose the equal distance = D km
Then, time taken with V1 and V2 speeds are; D/V1, D/V2 respectively. 
Avg Speed= 2D/ (D/V1+ D/V2)  
= 2V1*V2/ (V1+V2) = 4 km/hr 
In second case, average speed = V1+ V2/ 2  = 4.5 km/hr
So, using in above equations; V1 + V2 = 9    and   V1*V2 = 18
Now, (V1- V2)2 = (V1 +V2)2 – 4V1V2 = 81 - 72 
So, V1- V2= 3km/hr

m_Item1.QuestionText: Arun is traveling on his cycle and has calculated to reach point A at 2 pm if he travels at 10 kmph, he will reach there at 12 noon if he travels at 15 kmph. At what speed must he travel to reach A at 1 pm?
m_Item1.Explanation:
Let the distance be x km

if travels at 10 kmph, Arun will reach point A at 2 pm

if travels at 15 kmph, Arun will reach point 12 noon

=> Time taken when traveling at 10 km = Time taken when traveling at 15 km + 2 hours
= x/10 = x/15+2      = x = 60            = Distance = 60 km
Examine the any statement say, if travels at 10 kmph, Arun will reach point A at 2 pm

= Time taken = Distance/Speed = 60/10 = 6 hours
= He must have started 6 hours back 2 pm, ie, at 8 am
= Now he wants to reach at 1 pm. ie; time to be taken = 5 hours
= Speed needed = Distance/Time = 60/5   =12 kmph

m_Item1.QuestionText: Two boys start from the same place walking at the rate of 5 kmph and 5.5 kmph respectively in the same direction. What time will they take to be 8.5 km apart?
m_Item1.Explanation:
Relative speed = 5.5 - 5 = .5 kmph (because they walk in the same direction)

distance = 8.5 km
time = distance/speed = 8.5/.5  = 17 hr

m_Item1.QuestionText: A man completes a journey in 10 hours. He travels first half of the journey at the rate of 21 km/hr and second half at the rate of 24 km/hr. Find the total journey in km.
m_Item1.Explanation:
Let time taken to travel the first half = x hr 

then time taken to travel the second half = (10 - x) hr 


Distance covered in the first half = 21x
Distance covered in the second half = 24(10 - x)


But distance covered in the first half = Distance covered in the second half   
=> 21x = 24(10 - x)        =  x = 16/3
Hence Distance covered in the first half  = 21x   = 21 × 16/3   = 7×16 = 112 km
Total distance = 2×112   = 224 km

m_Item1.QuestionText: A person travelled 120km by steamer, 450 km by bus and 60 km by horse. It took 13 hours and 30 mins. If the speed of the bus is 3 times that of the horse and 1.5 times the steamer, find the speed of bus.
m_Item1.Explanation:
Suppose speed of horse = X km/hr. 
Then, speed of the bus = 3X and of steamer 2X 
Now, 120/2X + 450/3X + 60/X = 13.5
360+900+360= 13.5 * 6X      X= 20 
Speed of bus = 3X= 60 km/hr

m_Item1.QuestionText: A man leaves a point P at 6 am and reaches the point Q at 10 am. Another man leaves the point Q at 8 am and reaches the point P at 12 noon. At what time do they meet?
m_Item1.Explanation:
Let distance PQ is D km and they meet x hrs after the first man starts. 
Average speed of the first man = D/ 10-6 = D/4 km/hr
Average speed of the first man = D/ 12-8 = D/4 km/hr
Distance by first man =  Dx/4 km
They meet x hrs after the first man starts. 
The second man, as he starts 2 hrs late, meets after (x-2) hrs from his start. So, the distance travelled by second man = D(x-2)/4 km
Dx/4 + D(x-2)/4 km = D   
2x-2= D   So, x= 3hrs
So, the meet at 6am + 3m = 9am

m_Item1.QuestionText: A man walking at the rate of 5 km/hr crosses a bridge in 15 minutes. What is the length of the bridge (in metres)?
m_Item1.Explanation:
Speed = 5 km/hr            Time = 15 minutes = 1/4 hour
Length of the bridge = Distance Travelled by the man 
= Speed × Time              = 5 × 1/4 km        
= 5×1/4×1000 metre
=1250 metre

m_Item1.QuestionText: The distance between two stations A and B is 778 km. A train covers the journey from A to B at 84 km per hour and returns back to A with a uniform speed of 56 km per hour. Find the average speed of train during the whole journey.

Options.OptionText: 67.2 kmph

m_Item1.Explanation:
By using the formula of average speed = 2*a*b/(a+b)
 = 2* 84 * 56/ (84+ 56)
​​​​​​​= 67.2 kmph

m_Item1.QuestionText: A man goes to his office from his house at a speed of 3km/hr and returns at a speed of 2km/hr. If he takes 5hours in going and coming, what is the distance between his house and office?

The distance between two cities A and B is 330km. A train starts from A at 8a.m. and travels towards B at 60km/hr. Another train starts from B at 9a.m. and travels towards A at 75km/hr. At what time will they meet?

Two trains having length of 140metre and 160metre long run at the speed of 60km/hr and 40km/hr respectively in opposite directions (on parallel tracks). The time which they take to cross each other, is
A train runs at the speed of 72 kmph and crosses a 250 meter long platform in 26seconds. What is the length of the train?
m_Item1.Explanation:
Let the length of the train be x meter
72 *5/18 = (250+ x)/26
x = 270 m

m_Item1.QuestionText: Two trains, each 100metre long are moving in opposite directions. They cross each other in 8seconds. If one is moving twice as fast the other, the speed of the faster train is
m_Item1.Explanation:
Let the speed of the slower train be x m/sec

then, the speed of the faster train = 2x m/sec

Relative speed = (x+2x) m/sec = 3x m/sec

therefore, (100 +100)/8 = 3x

24x=200

x=25/3

so, the speed of the faster train is 50/3 m/sec

(50/3 x 18/5) km/hr

the speed of the faster train 60 kmph

m_Item1.QuestionText: A train, 800 metre long is running with a speed of 78 km/hr. It crosses a tunnel in 1 minute. What is the length of the tunnel (in metres)?
Three men start together to travel the same way around a circular track of 11Km. Their speeds are 4, 5.5, 8 Km per hour respectively. When will meet at the starting point ?
m_Item1.Explanation:
Explanation:
Time taken to complete one round= 11/4, 11/5.5, 11/8   = 11/4, 2/1, 11/8
LCM of 11/4, 2/1, 11/8 =( LCM of 11, 2, 11)/ (HCF of 4, 1, 8) = 22/1 = 22 Hrs 
They will meet after 22 hrs.

m_Item1.QuestionText: A person crosses a 600-metre-long street in 6 minutes. What is his speed in km per hour?
m_Item1.Explanation: S = (600/1000) / (6/60) km/h = 6 km/h

m_Item1.QuestionText: A bus travels at 54km/h while moving but after accounting for stopping time, to let passengers on and off the coach, it averages a speed of 45km/h. How many minutes does the bus stop for each hour?

Two cars start from X and Y and travel towards each other at the speed of 60 kmph and 80 kmph respectively. At the time of their meeting, the second car has travelled 160 km more than the first, the distance between A and B is?
m_Item1.Explanation:
The difference between the speeds is 20 kmph
Therefore the total time taken is 8 Hrs
distance covered by first car = 480 km
distance covered by second car = 640 km
Total distance= 1120 km 

OR
For same time, speed ratio = distance ratio =3/4

distance covered by second car = 4*160 km = 640 km
distance covered by first car =  3* 160 km = 480 km
Total distance= 1120 km

m_Item1.QuestionText: Walking at 5/8th of her usual speed, Akshita reaches her office 15 min late. Find her usual time.
m_Item1.Explanation:
Her speed ratio is 8:5 and hence time ratio is 5:8.

i.e. let his usual time is 5x and now taking 8x
 8x - 5x = 15 min
3x = 15 min
x = 5 min

Usual time is 5x = 25 min

m_Item1.QuestionText: A train 125 m long passes a man, running at 5 km/hr in the same direction in which the train is going, in 10 seconds. The speed of the train is :
A train running at the speed of 60 Km/hr crosses a pole in 9 seconds. What is the length of the train ?

m_Item1.QuestionText: A train can travel 50% faster than a car. Both start from point A at the same time and reach point B 75 kms away from A at the same time. On the way, however, the train lost about 12.5 minutes while stopping at the stations. The speed of the car is:

Options.OptionText: 100 <g class="gr_ gr_1443 gr-alert gr_spell gr_inline_cards gr_run_anim ContextualSpelling ins-del multiReplace" data-gr-id="1443" id="1443">kmph</g>
110 <g class="gr_ gr_1444 gr-alert gr_spell gr_inline_cards gr_run_anim ContextualSpelling" data-gr-id="1444" id="1444">kmph</g>
120 <g class="gr_ gr_1530 gr-alert gr_spell gr_inline_cards gr_run_anim ContextualSpelling ins-del multiReplace" data-gr-id="1530" id="1530">kmph</g>

m_Item1.QuestionText: Two trains are moving in opposite directions @ 60 km/hr and 90 km/hr. Their lengths are 1.10 km and 0.9 km respectively. The time taken by the slower train to cross the faster train in seconds is :
Two trains are running at 40 km/hr and 20 km/hr respectively in the same direction. Fast train completely passes a man sitting in the slower train in 5 seconds. What is the length of the fast train ?
A train moves at a constant speed of 120 km/hr for one km and at 40 km/hr for the next km. Find the average speed of the train.

Two trains starting at same time, one from Bangalore to Mysore and other in opposite direction arrive at their destination 1 hr and 4 hours respectively after passing each other. How much faster is one train from other?
m_Item1.Explanation:
Let's denote the distance between Bangalore and Mysore as d (in km).
The relative speed of the two trains is the sum of their speeds, as they are moving towards each other. So,
Relative speed=V1+V2
The time taken to meet is the time taken for the trains to cover the distance between them, which is d. So,
Time taken to meet= d/Relative speed
Given that one train arrives 1 hour after meeting and the other arrives 4 hours after meeting, we can set up two equations:
d=(v1 + v2)×1
​d=(v1 + v2)×4
d=v1+v2
Substitute this into the second equation:
v1+v2=4(v1+v2)
v1+v2=4v1+4v2
3v1=3v2
v1=v2
This means the speeds of the two trains are equal. Therefore, the correct answer is "Cannot be determined".

m_Item1.QuestionText: Walking at 3/4th of his regular speed, Sanjay is 16 minutes late in reaching the office. Find the usual time that he takes to reach the office.
A ship went on a voyage. After it had traveled 180 miles a plane started with 10 times the speed of the ship. Find the distance when they meet from starting point.
Air India flight IC-814 flies 930 kilometres in 75 minutes. How many kilometers it flies in 4 hours 45 minutes assuming a constant speed?
m_Item1.Explanation:
Speed = 930*60/75 = 744 kmph
Distance travelled in 4 hours 45 minutes = 744 * 4 + 744 * 3/4
                                                                         = 2976 + 558
                                                                          = 3534 km

m_Item1.QuestionText: There are 20 poles with a constant distance between each pole. A car takes 24 second to reach the 12th pole. How much will it take to reach the last pole.?

In a building, there are two adjacent elevators- one goes up and the other comes down. On the 53rd floor Ajay gets into the one going down at the rate of 42 floors per minute. On the 8th floor Vijay gets into the one going up at the rate of 48 floors per minute. On which floor will both elevators be at the same time, if both get into the elevators simultaneously?

The given question is followed by two statements. You have to decide which statement/s is/are sufficient to answer that question.
Rajdhani Express leaves Mumbai for Nagpur at 7:00 am. Can it reach Nagpur before 7:00 pm on the same day?
Statement I: The average speed of the Rajdhani express during the whole journey, including 4 halts of 15 minutes each, is less than 80 km/hr.
Statement II: The distance from Mumbai to Nagpur is 700 km.

Options.OptionText: If either alone statement is sufficient to answer the question.
If only one statement alone is sufficient but the other is not sufficient to answer the question.
If the question cannot be answered even after combining both the statements.
If both statements are required to answer the question.

m_Item1.Explanation: We cannot answer the question even after we combine both the statements.Because exact value of  speed is not given.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
The given question is followed by two statements. You have to decide which statement/s is/are sufficient to answer that question.
What is the respective speed of the two trains, A and B of length 200 meter and 240 meter respectively?
Statement I: They take 20 seconds to cross each other when they are running in opposite direction.
Statement II: They take 40 seconds to cross each other where running in the same direction.

Options.OptionText: If only one statement alone is sufficient but the other is not sufficient to answer the question.
If either alone statement is sufficient to answer the question.
If both statements are required to answer the question.
If the question cannot be answered even after combining both the statements.

m_Item1.Explanation: We need both statements to answer the question.

m_Item1.QuestionText: If a train of length 300 m and travelling at 90 km/hr passes a platform in 5/12 minutes, find length of the platform.
m_Item1.Explanation:
D= S*T
length of train + length of platform = 90 * (5/18) *(5/12)*60 = 25m/s * 25 sec
300 +length of platform =625 m
​​​​​​​length of platform = 325 m

m_Item1.QuestionText: A bus goes at an average speed of 75kmph without taking any halt. The same bus covers the same distance at an average speed of of 65kmph when it halts for equal minutes every hour. Find for how many minutes it halts every hour.
m_Item1.Explanation:
Stoppage time per hr = 10/75 = 2/15 hr.
​​​​​​​Every hour halt = 2/15 * 60 = 8 mins

m_Item1.QuestionText: Kunal travels one-third of his total planned distance at a speed of 15 km/hr and the remaining at 20 km/hr. Find the average speed of his entire journey.
m_Item1.Explanation: Average speed = total distance/total time.

m_Item1.QuestionText: 2.5 hours after a freight train leaves Pune, an express train leaves Pune travelling in the same direction at an average speed of 40 km/hr on a different track. After travelling for 1.5 hr the express train overtakes the freight train. Find the average speed of the freight train.
m_Item1.Explanation: Freight train took 4 hours to travel 40*1.5 = 60 km. Hence, its speed is 15 kmph.

m_Item1.QuestionText: If David drives at 20% higher speed for 10% more time, how much percent more distance he would cover?
m_Item1.Explanation:
Use Successive percent change.
(20+ 10+20*10/100)% = (30+2)% = 32 %

OR

d= s * t
new d = 1.2 s * 1.1 t = 1.32 st = 1.32 d

m_Item1.QuestionText: Two friends, Arun and Tarun stay on a same street but 1.5 km away from each other. One day, they decided to meet at a café which is in between their residences. They started walking towards the café at 7:10 pm. Arun met with a friend on the way. He spoke to him for half a minute and then continued towards café. They both reached café at same time. If Arun walks at 5 m/s and Tarun walks at 6 m/s, find at what time they reached the café.
m_Item1.Explanation: Let the café is at a distance of x m from Arun’s house. He took x/5 + 30 seconds to reach there. Tarun took (1500-x)/6 seconds to reach there. Equating the two, we get x = 600. Hence, Arun took 600/5 + 30 = 150 seconds to reach the café. Both reached café at same time - 7:12:30 pm.

m_Item1.QuestionText: By walking at 8/11th of her usual speed, Shalaka is late by 30 minutes. Find her usual time.
m_Item1.Explanation:
As distance is same, we can equate the distance.
We have s1*t = (8/11) s1*(t+30).
Solving this, t = 80 min.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In how many minutes, a train 240 m long will pass a telegraphic pole, if speed of the train is 50 km/hr?
m_Item1.Explanation: The time taken in minutes = 240*18/ (50*5*60) = 0.28

m_Item1.QuestionText: A man by motorcycle goes from Delhi to Bharatpur, a distance of 192 km, at an average speed of 32 kmph. Another man starts from Delhi by car 2.5 h after the motorcyclist starts and reaches Bharatpur half an hour late. What is the ratio of person on the motorcycle to the person going by car?

A tiger is 50 of its own leaps behind a deer. The tiger takes 5 leaps per minutes to deer’s 4. If the tiger and the deer cover 8 m and 5 m per leap respectively, what distance will the tiger have to run before it catches the deer?

Ashish Travels 760 km to his home, partly by train and partly by car He takes 8 hours, if he travels 160 km by train and the rest by car. He takes 12minuts more, if he travels 240 km by train and the rest by car. What are the speed of the train and of the car?

Options.OptionText: Speed of car = 90 km/h, speed of train = 60 km/h
Speed of car = 100 km/h, speed of train = 80 km/h
Speed of car = 80 km/h, speed of train = 70 km/h
Speed of car = 100 km/h, speed of train = 90 km/h

m_Item1.QuestionText: A train can travel 50% faster than a car. Both start from point A at the same time and reach point B 75 kms away from A at the same time. On the way, however, the train lost about 12.5 minutes while stopping at the stations. The speed of the car is:
Sushant walks 6 km at a speed of 1 1/2 kmph, runs 8 km at a speed of 2 kmph and goes by bus another 32 km. Speed of the bus is 8 kmph. Find the average speed of the man.

A train starts from Delhi at 6:00 a.m. and reaches Meerut at 10 a.m. The other train starts from Meerut at 8 a.m. and reaches Delhi at 11:30 a.m. If the distance between Delhi and Meerut is 200 km, then at what time did the two trains meet each other?

The Delhi Express runs @ 144 kilometres per hours and it reaches destination 20 minute ahead of schedule. If it averages 100 km/hour, it reaches 24 minutes behind schedule. What is the average speed it should maintain in order to reach the destination on schedule?

Two girls move in opposite directions, one from A to B and other from B to A. The girl from A reaches the destination in 16 hrs and girl from B reaches her destination in 25 hrs, after having met. If former's speed is 25 km/hr, what will be the speed of latter?

An aeroplane flying 1000 km covers the first 200 km at the rate of 200 km/hr, the second 200 km at 400 km/hr, the third 200 km at 600 km / hr & last 200 km at the rate of 800 km/hr. Determine the average speed of the aeroplane.

In how much time, two trains running at 48 km/hr and 72 km/hr will take to cross each other completely if they are 2 km apart? Given that length of the faster train is 350 meter and that of the slower train is 450 meter.
m_Item1.Explanation:
The total distance to be covered by two trains is 2000 m + 350 m + 450 m = 2800 m.
As they are running in opposite directions, relative speed will be 120 km/hr.
Hence, time taken will be 2800/ (120*5/18) seconds = 84 seconds.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Sumedh goes 20% faster and covers 12% more distance. By what percent he reduces his time?
m_Item1.Explanation: Using successive percentage change, we get 12 = 20 + b + 20b100 . By solving this, we get b = -6.66%. That means, time is reduced by 6.66%.

m_Item1.QuestionText: A train A overtakes another train B running at 80 km/hr in one and half minute. Train A passes a man who is riding a bicycle at 15 km/hr in opposite direction in 16 seconds. Find length of the train A, if that of train B is 400 m.
m_Item1.Explanation: We have 90 = (LA+400)/((SA-80)*5/18) and 16 = LA/((SA+15)*5/18). Solving these two equations, we get LA = 600.

m_Item1.QuestionText: A train passes a platform of length 420 m in 50 seconds. It passes a tree in 20 seconds. Find length of the train.
m_Item1.Explanation: From given information, it is clear than the train passes only platform in 50-20 = 30 seconds. It passes itself in 20 seconds. It means if it covers 420 m in 30 seconds, it will cover 280 m in 20 seconds.

m_Item1.QuestionText: A train after travelling 50 km meet with an accident and then proceeds at 3/4th of its former speed and arrives at its destination 35 minutes late. Had an accident occurred 24 kms further, it would have reached destination only 25 minutes late. The speed of the train is ?
m_Item1.Explanation:
Train proceeds at 3/4 S where S is his usual speed means 1/4 decrease in the speed which will lead to 1/3 increase in the time.
[ As S *T = D, Speed is inversely proportional to time when distance is constant].
Now, the main difference come in those 24 km and the change in difference of time = 35 - 25 = 10 m
= 1 / 3 *T = 10 where T is the time required to cover the distance of (50 - 74) = 24 km
T = 30 min = 0.5 hours
Speed of the train = 24 / (0.5) = 48 kmph.

m_Item1.QuestionText: If Suresh, Ramesh and Mahesh travel the same distance and ratio of their times to travel that distance is 8:6:9, what is the ratio of their speeds?
m_Item1.Explanation:
Go by options. Take the product of respective speed and time values. If distance is same in all three pairs, that is the answer. For example, for option B: 8*9 = 6*12 = 9*8.

Or
Since time and speed are inversely proportional we can find the inverse ratio of 8:6:9
which is 1/8:1/6:1/9
LCM of 8,6,9 = 72 
Multiplying 72
72 *1/8: 72*1/6: 72*1/9

​​​​​​​= 9: 12: 8

m_Item1.QuestionText: A cyclist covers a distance of 750 m in 2 min 30 sec. What is the speed in km/hr of the cyclist.
m_Item1.Explanation:
dist. = 750 m
time = 2 min 30 sec = 150 sec
speed = dist/time = 750/150 = 5 m/s
speed = 5 * 18/5 km/hr = 18 kn/hr

m_Item1.QuestionText: A train 360 m long is running at speed of 45 km/hr. In what time will it pass a bridge 140 m long.
m_Item1.Explanation:
Speed = 45 * 5/18 m/sec = 25/2 m/sec
Total dist. covered = 360 + 140 m = 500 m
Required time = 500 * 2/25 sec = 40 sec.

m_Item1.QuestionText: A man sitting in a train which is traveling at 50 km/hr observes that a goods train travelling in opposite direction, takes 9 sec. to pass him. If the goods train is 280 m long ,find its speed.
m_Item1.Explanation:
Required speed = 280/9 m/sec = 280/ 9 * 18/5  km/h = 112 km/hr
since 112 km/hr is the relative speed
speed of goods train = (112 - 50 )km/hr = 62 km/hr

m_Item1.QuestionText: How many minuts does Aditya take to cover a distance of 400m , if he runs at speed of 20 km/hr?
m_Item1.Explanation:
distance = 400 m
speed = 20 km/hr = 20 * 5/18 = 50/9 m/s
time = distance/speed = 400 * 9/50 = 72 sec = 1 (12/60) = 1 (1/5) min

m_Item1.QuestionText: A man walks at the speed of 5 kmph and runs at the speed of 10 kmph. How much time will the man require to cover the distance of 28 km, if he covers half (first 14 km) his journey walking and half his journey running?
m_Item1.Explanation:
Time taken to cover the first half (14 km) walking:
Time = Distance / Speed = 14 km / 5 kmph = 2.8 hours

Time taken to cover the second half (also 14 km) running:
Time = Distance / Speed = 14 km / 10 kmph = 1.4 hours

Total time = Time walking + Time running
= 2.8 hours + 1.4 hours
= 4.2 hours

m_Item1.QuestionText: A thief spots a policeman 100 m away and takes to his heels. If the police man gives a chase immediately, then how far would the thief have run before he is over taken? The speeds of the theif and policeman are 8 kmph and 10 kmph respectively.
m_Item1.Explanation:
Distance between policeman and thief = 100 m
Speed of the thief = 8 km/hr
Speed of the policeman = 10 km/hr
Relative speed = Speed of policeman - Speed of thief
Relative speed = 10 km/hr - 8 km/hr = 2 km/hr

Converting relative speed to m/s:
Relative speed = 2×5/18m/s = 10/18 m/s = 5/9

Time taken by the police to overtake the thief = Distance / Relative speed
Time taken = 100/5/9=100×9/5sec = 180 sec

Distance travelled by the thief in 180 sec = Speed of thief × Time
Distance = 180 x 8×5/18
​Distance = 400 meters.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Champaklal covers 25 kms at the speed 15 kmph and other 15 kms at speed of 5 kmph. What is his average speed throughout this journey?
m_Item1.Explanation:
Time = Distance / Speed
T1 = 25 / 15 = 5 / 3 hr
T2 = 15 / 5 = 3 hr
Total Time = 5/3 + 3 = 14/3
Total distance = 25+15 = 40
Average speed = Total distance / Total Time = 40 * 3/14 = 8.56(Aproximately is equal to 8 kmph).

m_Item1.QuestionText: A train is moving at a sped of 132 km/hr. If the length of the train is 110 meter , how long will it take to cross a railway platform 165 meter long?
m_Item1.Explanation:
Convert the speed in m/sec => 132 * 5/18 m/sec = 110/3 m/sec

Distance covered in passing the platform = (110+165) m = 275 m
​​​​​​​
Time taken = (275 * 3/110) sec = 15/2 sec 7 1/2 sec.

m_Item1.QuestionText: A can complete a journey in 10 hrs. He travels first half of the journey at the rate of 21 km/hr and second half at the rate of 24 km/hr. Find the total journey in km.
m_Item1.Explanation:
Total distance be x km
Then first half = x/2/21 = x/2*21
Then second half = x/2/24 = x/2*24
x/21 + x/24 = 20
x/7 +x/8 = 60
x = 224 km

m_Item1.QuestionText: A person travels from P to Q at speed of 40 km/hr and returns by increasing speed by 150%. What is his average speed for both the trips?
m_Item1.Explanation:
forword speed =  40 km/hr
return speed  = 1.5*40+40 = 100 km/hr
Average speed = 2*40*100 / 40+100 = 8000/140 = 57.14 km/hr

m_Item1.QuestionText: Raj travels 360 km on three-fifths of his petrol tank. How far would he travel at the same rate with a full tank of petrol?
m_Item1.Explanation:
Distance travelled by Raj with three-fifths (i.e. ⅗) of petrol tank = 360 km
Distance travelled by him with a full petrol tank = (360 ÷ 3/5) km
= (360 x 5)/3 km
= 120 x 5 km
= 600 km.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In how much time, a train of length 400 m running at 72 km/hr will pass a platform of length 500 m?

Two cities P &Q are 275 km apart. A rider starts from P towards Q at 11 a.m. at the speed of 20 km/hr. Another rider starts from Q towards P at 12 noon at the speed of 25 km/hr. Find at what time they will cross each other?
The scale of a map is 1:50000. If the actual distance between two places is 5 km, what is the distance on the map in cm?
m_Item1.Explanation: 5 km = 5000 meters = 500000 cm. At a scale of 1:50000, the map distance is 500000/50000 = 10 cm.

m_Item1.QuestionText: A car travels a distance of 150 km in 3 hours and returns to the starting point in 2 hours. What is the average speed of the car for the entire journey?
m_Item1.Explanation: Total distance = 150 km + 150 km = 300 km. Total time = 3 hours + 2 hours = 5 hours. Average speed = Total distance / Total time = 300 km / 5 hours = 60 km/h.

m_Item1.QuestionText: A cyclist covers a distance of 48 km at a constant speed. If the cyclist had traveled 4 km/h faster, the journey would have taken 1 hour less. What was the original speed of the cyclist?
m_Item1.Explanation: Let the original speed be x km/h. The time taken at the original speed is 48/x hours. At the new speed (x + 4) km/h, the time taken is 48/(x + 4) hours. According to the problem, (48/x) - (48/(x + 4)) = 1. Solving, we get x = 12 km/h.

m_Item1.QuestionText: A train running at a speed of 60 km/h takes 7 seconds to pass a pole. What is the length of the train?
m_Item1.Explanation: The length of the train can be calculated using the formula: Length = Speed × Time. Convert speed to m/s by multiplying with 5/18. Speed = 60 × 5/18 = 50/3 m/s. Length = (50/3) × 7 = 350/3 = 116.67 meters (approximately 117 meters).

m_Item1.QuestionText: A train overtakes two persons walking in the same direction at 2 km/h and 4 km/h respectively and passes them completely in 9 and 10 seconds respectively. What is the length of the train?
m_Item1.Explanation: Let the speed of the train be V km/h. For the first person: Distance = (V - 2) × (5/18) × 9, and for the second person: Distance = (V - 4) × (5/18) × 10. Equating both equations and solving for V gives the length of train = 90 meters.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Two girls started walking from opposite ends of a straight track, each at a speed of 4 km/hr. If they started at the same time and meet after 2 hours, what was the distance between them at the beginning?
m_Item1.Explanation: Both girls walk at 4 km/hr for 2 hours each. Since they started at opposite ends and meet, they cover a combined distance of 4 km/hr * 2 + 4 km/hr * 2 = 16 km.

m_Item1.QuestionText: A car travels at a speed of 60 km/h and reaches its destination in 5 hours. How far is the destination?
m_Item1.Explanation: Distance = Speed × Time = 60 km/h × 5 h = 300 km

m_Item1.QuestionText: If a train travels a distance of 450 km in 5 hours, what is its average speed?
m_Item1.Explanation: Average Speed = Total Distance / Total Time = 450 km / 5 h = 90 km/h

m_Item1.QuestionText: A cyclist covers a distance of 96 km in 8 hours. What is the average speed of the cyclist?
m_Item1.Explanation: Average Speed = Total Distance / Total Time = 96 km / 8 h = 12 km/h

m_Item1.QuestionText: A person walks at a speed of 5 km/h and covers a certain distance in 4 hours. What is the distance covered?
m_Item1.Explanation: Distance = Speed × Time = 5 km/h × 4 h = 20 km

m_Item1.QuestionText: If a car covers 300 km in 3 hours, what is its speed in km/h?
m_Item1.Explanation: Speed = Distance / Time = 300 km / 3 h = 100 km/h

m_Item1.QuestionText: A runner completes a marathon of 42 km in 6 hours. What is the runner's average speed?
m_Item1.Explanation: Average Speed = Total Distance / Total Time = 42 km / 6 h = 7 km/h

m_Item1.QuestionText: Two cities are 120 km apart. If a car travels from one city to another in 2 hours, what is its speed?
m_Item1.Explanation: Speed = Distance / Time = 120 km / 2 h = 60 km/h

m_Item1.QuestionText: A train running at a speed of 60 km/hr takes 9 seconds to cross a platform of length 200 meters. What is the length of the train?
m_Item1.Explanation: Speed = 60 km/hr = (60 * 1000) / 3600 m/s = 50/3 m/s. Distance = speed * time = (50/3) * 9 = 150 meters.

m_Item1.QuestionText: A train covers a distance of 600 meters in 24 seconds. What is its speed in km/hr?
m_Item1.Explanation: Speed = Distance / Time = 600 / 24 m/s = 25 m/s. Speed in km/hr = 25 * (18/5) = 90 km/hr.

m_Item1.QuestionText: A train running at 54 km/hr takes 20 seconds to pass a bridge. If the length of the train is 120m, what is the length of the bridge?
m_Item1.Explanation: Speed = 54 km/hr = (54 * 1000) / 3600 m/s = 15 m/s. Distance = Speed * Time = 15 * 20 = 300 meters. Length of bridge = 300 - 120 = 180 meters.

m_Item1.QuestionText: A train travels from Station A to Station B at a speed of 60 km/hr and returns to Station A at a speed of 90 km/hr. What is the average speed of the train for the entire journey?
m_Item1.Explanation: The average speed for a round trip is given by the formula: \( \text{Average Speed} = \frac{2xy}{x + y} \). Here, x = 60 km/hr and y = 90 km/hr. \( \text{Average Speed} = \frac{2 \times 60 \times 90}{60 + 90} = \frac{10800}{150} = 72 \text{ km/hr} \).

m_Item1.QuestionText: A train traveling at a speed of 72 km/h takes 18 seconds to pass completely through a station. What is the length of the train?
m_Item1.Explanation: The train's speed in meters per second is 72 km/h = 72 × (1000/3600) = 20 m/s. If it takes 18 seconds to pass through the station, the length of the train is Speed × Time = 20 m/s × 18 s = 360 meters.

m_Item1.QuestionText: A train 450 meters long crosses a pole in 15 seconds. What is the speed of the train in km/h?
m_Item1.Explanation: The speed of the train can be calculated using Speed = Distance / Time. Speed in meters per second = 450 m / 15 s = 30 m/s. Converting to km/h: 30 × (3600/1000) = 108 km/h.
